Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 406 upvotes, 94 comments
  2. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 139 upvotes, 32 comments
  3. George Goble has died 86 upvotes, 18 comments
  4. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 975 upvotes, 305 comments
  5. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 394 upvotes, 135 comments
  6. Foxing aspires to be an eBPF-powered replication engine for Linux filesystems 26 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 76 upvotes, 22 comments
  8. Hugo's New CSS Powers 33 upvotes, 7 comments
  9. Prefer do notation over Applicative operators when assembling records (2024) 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1502 upvotes, 664 comments
  11. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 307 upvotes, 135 comments
  12. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 49 upvotes, 15 comments
  13. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 114 upvotes, 46 comments
  14. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 240 upvotes, 110 comments
  15. JSON Canvas Spec (2024) 76 upvotes, 29 comments
  16. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 158 upvotes, 71 comments
  17. Tailscale's new macOS home 243 upvotes, 116 comments
  18. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 706 upvotes, 372 comments
  19. Yggdrasil Network 80 upvotes, 32 comments
  20. Amazon is adding a fuel surcharge to fees it collects from third-party sellers 117 upvotes, 53 comments
  21. Significant raise of reports 264 upvotes, 143 comments
  22. Show HN: A P2P messenger with dual network modes (Fast and Tor) 23 upvotes, 8 comments
  23. Memo: A language that remembers only the last 12 lines of code 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 257 upvotes, 166 comments
  25. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 265 upvotes, 208 comments
  26. Cursor 3 230 upvotes, 192 comments
  27. The Australian government has announced gambling advertising reforms 52 upvotes, 36 comments
  28. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 121 upvotes, 100 comments
  29. Magic the Gathering Deck Shuffler 26 upvotes, 15 comments
  30. ParadeDB (YC S23) Is Hiring Database Internal Engineers (Rust) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 7:00am, 3 April 2026. Source here.
